title: Chest pain in a 58-year-old male
presentation: A 58-year-old male with a history of hypertension and type 2 diabetes presents to the ED with 45 minutes of crushing substernal chest pain radiating to the left arm. He is diaphoretic and anxious. Vitals: BP 158/94, HR 96, RR 18, SpO2 97% on room air, Temp 37.1C. On exam: JVD present, lung fields clear.

gold_standard_workup.key_decision_point: ST elevation in leads II, III, aVF = inferior STEMI, activate cath lab
